mechanism_narrative: Rising retail and food service sales indicate robust consumer spending, which directly boosts corporate revenues and earnings expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The Spearman rank correlation between month-over-month RSAFS changes and subsequent 20-day SPX returns is not significantly positive (p < 0.05) over any rolling 3-year window, or the proportion of positive forward returns following RSAFS increases is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8%, the economy is in significant distress, reducing consumer confidence and spending, which depresses corporate profits and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The average 20-day forward SPX return in periods when UNRATE > 8% is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05), or the hit rate of negative returns in such periods is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When the real federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS minus CPI inflation) is negative, monetary policy is accommodative, stimulating borrowing, investment, and economic growth, which supports positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The average 20-day forward SPX return in months with negative real rates is not significantly positive (p > 0.05) compared to months with positive real rates, or the strategy yields negative returns in more than 45% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: During the early recovery phase after a severe recession, rising nonfarm payrolls confirm sustainable economic healing, which boosts investor confidence and drives positive long-term equity returns, whereas similar payroll gains in late-cycle regimes have weaker predictive power.
failure_condition: The difference in mean forward 60-day SPX return following positive PAYEMS changes between early-recovery and late-cycle regimes is not statistically significant (two-sample t-test p > 0.10), or the early-recovery regime does not show a positive hit rate above 60%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in industrial production signals a sudden acceleration in manufacturing activity, which is quickly reflected in higher earnings expectations and drives immediate positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: The proportion of 5-day forward SPX returns that are positive following a triggering INDPRO increase is not significantly above 55% (binomial test p > 0.05), or the average return is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in consumer price inflation reduces real purchasing power and signals future monetary tightening, which increases discount rates and depresses equity valuations, leading to negative forward returns.
failure_condition: The Spearman correlation between monthly CPIAUCSL changes and subsequent 20-day SPX returns is not significantly negative over any 3-year rolling window, or the proportion of negative forward returns following CPIAUCSL increases is below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous gains in nonfarm payrolls and retail sales indicate a virtuous cycle of employment and consumption, which amplifies earnings growth and creates a powerful long-term positive signal for equity markets.
failure_condition: When both proxies rise faster than their respective rolling 6-month medians, the average 60-day forward SPX return is not significantly positive (p > 0.05), or the interaction term in a regression model is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In the post-housing-crisis recovery, rising housing starts are a unique and powerful signal of healing in residential investment, which spills over to broader economic activity and drives equity prices; this effect weakens once construction normalizes.
failure_condition: The predictive performance of HOUST changes for 60-day forward SPX returns is not significantly higher during the 2009-2012 recovery period compared to the 2005-2008 period, or the hit rate in the recovery period is below 55%.
